reasoning: The Fed has been reducing its Treasury holdings by up to $25B per month under its QT program. On March 19, 2025, the Fed announced it would lower this runoff cap to $5B per month, effective April 1. The baseline date of March 30, 2025, precedes the March 31 end-of-month Treasuries maturity date, meaning the first resolution date (April 6) will reflect this significant March runoff (likely $10B-$15B). Thus, the initial dates are virtually guaranteed to be lower than March 30. From April through late 2025, the balance will continue shrinking at about $5B a month, pushing probabilities for April, June, and September very low. However, by March 2026, QT is expected to have completely ended, and the Fed is likely to have resumed organic balance sheet growth to keep up with the growth in currency in circulation (often ~$10B/month). It is quite possible that organic growth by Q1 2026 outweighs the small remaining QT runoff, making an increase by March 2026 possible, though uncertain.
